item.title: China’s forgotten consumers
item.description: Plus more on private credit and BDCs

item.title: Trump’s Iran folly is handing Xi Jinping weapons in the trade war
item.description: Despite China being a huge oil importer, US mismanagement will give Beijing the upper hand in upcoming talks

item.title: Should you lie to your kids about their Junior Isas?
item.description: Parents worry about their children getting access to a lump sum at an early age — some choose to keep it a secret

item.title: Which chokepoint wins in a game of geoeconomic Top Trumps?
item.description: It depends on its damage to the target, potential for blowback and durability

item.title: America risks financial credibility with payments deficit obsession
item.description: Imposing tariffs in response threatens to create a problem where none exists

item.title: UK’s water utilities may finally be worth a dip
item.description: Despite a recent price bump, valuations are still relatively low

item.title: Europe needs to stop bickering to compete with China
item.description: The fight over ‘Made in Europe’ versus ‘Made with Europe’ is a pointless one

item.title: Nasdaq finds SpaceX’s gravitational pull hard to resist
item.description: Proposed change to index-inclusion policy would benefit Elon Musk ahead of IPO

item.title: Turbulence tests South Korea’s stock market revival
item.description: Sharp swings are a reminder to Seoul that its reform agenda is incomplete

item.title: In LA’s ‘Tehrangeles’, Trump’s war is popular
item.description: The largest community outside Iran danced in the streets but diverges with the president on a return to monarchy

item.title: Trump’s revolutionary sense of time is changing politics
item.description: The US president doesn’t think in terms of long-term strategy but rather in terms of deadlines

item.title: China and Russia are capturing the global space sector
item.description: Beijing and Moscow are exploiting the extraplanetary ambitions of less developed countries

item.title: The lucrative private equity pay scheme under challenge
item.description: Shareholders are challenging a contentious form of financial engineering that rewards founders

item.title: Farage forgets which country he aims to govern
item.description: In domestic and foreign policy, the British right’s vulnerability is its America-worship

item.title: Oil fears hit Asian stocks harder than profits
item.description: Reaction reflects possibility of sustained supply disruption and prolonged surge in crude prices

item.title: Does private credit have a credit quality problem?
item.description: The stock market thinks so

item.title: The economic consequences of war with Iran
item.description: There are lasting lessons about energy resilience to be learnt from this conflict

item.title: Is intergenerational inequality unfair?
item.description: Sloppy assumptions do not help Millennials — or their Baby Boomer detractors

item.title: UBS’s new private equity deals confront an old problem
item.description: A firm should think carefully about which risks are worthwhile and tolerable to their clients

item.title: The usefulness of useless knowledge
item.description: Politicians aren’t the best judges of the merits of scientific research

item.title: How to fight deepfakes
item.description: People are now little better than chance at telling what’s real and what’s not

item.title: Rising sulphur prices carry whiff of unintended consequences
item.description: Global efforts to phase out fossil fuels will erode supply of cheap sulphur over time

item.title: How can I get my money out of a third-party account?
item.description: The return on my investment hasn’t materialised and I’m worried there may be fraud on the account

item.title: Ask a stylist: how do you pack for business travel?
item.description: Do your pre-trip homework, focus on neutrals — and take nothing that isn’t multifunctional

item.title: No snow, no refund: weird weather tests Big Ski’s business model
item.description: Vail’s attempt to lock in revenue with its annual pass has come adrift because of rising prices amid less of the white stuff
